mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and lifting equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.5%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, leading to higher profit margins and positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising consumer prices erode real purchasing power and force tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and depressing forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3% monthly threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward SPX returns over a rolling 10-year window, or the predictive R-squared drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When employment levels surge past their 12-month moving average, it signals accelerating economic activity and consumer demand, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the monthly employment growth threshold fails to maintain a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in retail sales coinciding with stagnant federal funds rates indicates strong consumer spending supported by accommodative credit conditions, fueling equity outperformance over the following month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of rising short-term rates.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained environments, rising industrial production fails to keep pace with demand, triggering cost-push inflation that squeezes corporate margins and depresses equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth < 0.2% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2%
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically neutral when industrial production stagnates alongside rising consumer prices, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
